result:
Done. The three missing operations are implemented in `CsvRow.calr` in the Calor DSL, following the conventions of the existing `NeedsQuoting` fixture (inline `(type:name) -> ret` signatures, sequential `f00N` IDs, explicit effect declarations), and the project builds cleanly. This took 2 of the 10 build cycles (the first failed because Calor's effect enforcement requires `SplitRow` to declare `§E{mut}` for its local list `§PUSH` mutations — everything else compiled first try).

What was added:

- **`EscapeField`** (`f002`) — if `NeedsQuoting(field)`, doubles internal quotes via `replace` and wraps the result in quotes; otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** (`f003`) — builds the line with a StringBuilder, iterating fields with a first-element flag so commas only separate (an empty list yields the empty string).
- **`SplitRow`** (`f004`) — a character-scanning while loop with an `inQuotes` state: doubled quotes inside a quoted field emit one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside quotes are content, a comma outside quotes ends the field, and the final buffer is always pushed (so the empty line gives one empty field and a trailing comma gives a trailing empty field).

Beyond the clean build, I verified behavior end-to-end with a temporary console harness (created outside the project and deleted afterward) that referenced the built `Src.dll`: 16 directed cases covering every spec rule (empty line, trailing comma, quoted commas, doubled quotes, CR/LF in quotes) plus 5,000 randomized `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trips over an adversarial alphabet of quote/comma/newline-heavy fields — all passed.
